qa_pairs.question: What is the primary function of the default security group for a virtual machine?
qa_pairs.answer: The default security group enables a virtual machine (VM) to access the internet for tasks such as downloading operating system upgrades or installing packages. It also facilitates communication among other VMs within the same default security group, while preventing access from external machines.

qa_pairs.question: Why is it recommended not to remove rules from the default security group?
qa_pairs.answer: It is recommended not to remove rules from the default security group because doing so may cause problems when creating new virtual machines (VMs).

qa_pairs.question: What types of rules are typically included in a default security group?
qa_pairs.answer: A default security group typically includes two Egress rules, one for IPv4 and one for IPv6, to allow the virtual machine access to an outside network. It also includes two Ingress rules, one for IPv4 and one for IPv6, to permit communication among all virtual machines belonging to that same security group.
